us-politics.headline: Supreme Court Clears Path for Bannon and Trump Pardon Cases
us-politics.body:
The Supreme Court cleared the way Monday for dismissal of Steve Bannon's contempt of Congress conviction, following Trump's pardon of the former White House strategist. The court took similar action in the case of a former Cincinnati city councilman convicted of bribery, also pardoned by Trump. Both decisions send cases back to lower courts rather than resolving the underlying legal questions.

Critics argue the rulings illustrate how presidential pardons can short-circuit accountability for allies. Supporters counter that pardons are a legitimate constitutional tool and that the courts are simply following established law. The court also returned a separate state-secrets privilege case to a lower court, adding to a busy term for judicial watchers.

sources.title: Supreme Court returns state-secrets privilege case to lower court
Supreme Court clears the way for Bannon contempt case to be dismissed
Supreme Court clears way for dismissal in case of ex-Cincinnati councilman pardoned by Trump
Supreme Court paves way for dismissal of Steve Bannon contempt of Congress case

us-politics.headline: Trump Presses Iran as Ceasefire Draft Circulates
us-politics.body:
President Trump held press conferences Monday ahead of a deadline he set for Iran to open the Strait of Hormuz, raising pressure on Tehran over its nuclear program. Reports emerged that both the US and Iran have received a draft ceasefire proposal, though details remain sparse and no deal has been confirmed.

Opinion is sharply divided on Trump's approach. Some analysts warn that concentrating nuclear decision-making in a single executive is dangerous. Others argue that direct pressure has historically moved Iran toward negotiation. The situation remains fluid, with diplomats watching closely whether the deadline produces any concrete movement.

sources.title: Live updates: Trump to hold press conferences as deadline nears for Iran to open Strait of Hormuz
Watch live: Trump briefs press ahead of deadline on Iran
US, Iran receive draft ceasefire proposal: Reports
Nuclear war shouldn’t come down to just one person — especially when it’s Trump

us-politics.headline: Democrats Target State Legislatures, Eye Midterm Advantage
us-politics.body:
The Democratic Legislative Campaign Committee announced its first slate of targeted Republican state legislative seats ahead of November, signaling an early ground-level push to flip chambers in key states. Separately, Democratic strategists argue the party holds a structural edge heading into the midterms, pointing to historical patterns of opposition-party gains and Republican headwinds on economic issues.

Republicans dispute that framing, noting strong turnout infrastructure and Trump's continued influence over the base. A separate debate is brewing inside Democratic circles over the political power of podcasters and independent media, with some warning the party has ceded cultural ground to right-leaning online voices. The Hasan Piker controversy in Michigan's Senate primary underscores those tensions.

sources.title: Democrats announce first slate of state legislative targets ahead of November
Democrats face new reality: Podcasters’ political power
Democrats still have the edge heading into the Midterms
Hasan Piker controversy heats up Michigan Senate primary for Democrats

us-politics.headline: California Rail Costs Balloon; Trump Backs Hilton for Governor
us-politics.body:
California's high-speed rail project is now projected to cost $126 billion, a state official acknowledged, with no track yet laid. The figure draws fresh scrutiny to Governor Gavin Newsom's stewardship of the long-troubled project and hands critics a concrete target ahead of the 2026 gubernatorial race.

Trump moved quickly to shape that race, endorsing former Fox News host Steve Hilton on Monday. Trump called Hilton a future great governor, a signal that Republicans intend to compete seriously in a state they have long struggled to win statewide. Newsom, widely seen as a potential future presidential candidate, faces pressure from both the rail controversy and a well-funded, Trump-backed challenger.

sources.title: Trump endorses former Fox News host Steve Hilton in California governor race
Newsom's California rail project now expected to cost $126B, official admits, with still no tracks laid
President Trump makes endorsement in California gubernatorial race: 'He will be a GREAT Governor'

au-politics.headline: Fuel Crisis Bites Households, Businesses and Outback Tourism
au-politics.body:
Australia's fuel crisis is deepening. Diesel prices remain volatile despite Labor's fuel tax relief measures, with analysts warning the cuts have not translated into stable prices at the bowser. The Guardian's live tracker shows ongoing supply disruptions, service station outages and thin reserve margins across the country.

The human cost is visible in regional Australia. An outback pub near the Pink Roadhouse recorded just two visitors this Easter, with operators directly blaming fuel costs for keeping travellers home. The national auction clearance rate also hit its lowest point since 2022 over the weekend, adding to a broader picture of consumer pressure.

The right-leaning Spectator has framed the crisis as a symptom of government mismanagement, while Guardian reporting focuses on structural supply failures. Both sides agree prices are hurting Australians. They disagree sharply on who is responsible.

sources.title: Diesel remains volatile as prices rise again despite Labor’s fuel tax relief
Outback tourist pub had two visitors this Easter amid fuel price pinch
Track current petrol and diesel prices, service station outages and shipments – Australia's fuel crisis in charts
National auction clearance rate hits lowest point since 2022 – as it happened

au-politics.headline: Cyclone Maila Threatens Far North Queensland After Narelle's Destruction
au-politics.body:
A second severe tropical cyclone is bearing down on far north Queensland just three weeks after Cyclone Narelle caused widespread damage to the region. Cyclone Maila is tracking toward the coast, raising alarm among emergency services and communities still recovering from the previous event.

The back-to-back threat underscores the pressure on disaster response infrastructure in the state. Separately, a man's body was recovered after he was swept away in outback floodwaters near Innamincka, and a toddler died after being pulled from water on a Western Downs property. Queensland authorities are managing multiple concurrent emergencies.

No political dispute has emerged over the cyclone response yet, but the frequency of extreme weather events is likely to renew debate about climate resilience funding ahead of the federal election cycle.

sources.title: Severe Tropical Cyclone Maila could hit far north Queensland three weeks after Narelle tore through
Toddler dies after being pulled from water on Queensland property
Man’s body found after being swept away in outback flood waters

au-politics.headline: Hanson Preferences and Jury Trial Debate Stir Political Commentary
au-politics.body:
The Spectator Australia has published a favourable analysis of Pauline Hanson's One Nation, arguing the party is well-positioned to direct preferences in a way that could meaningfully shape the next Senate. The piece reflects growing right-wing interest in minor party influence as major party primary votes remain soft.

A separate Spectator commentary argues against moves to scrap jury trials in certain cases, framing it as an erosion of civil liberties. The piece does not attribute the push to a specific government but criticises the direction of legal reform broadly. Left-leaning outlets have not prominently covered either issue in the past 24 hours, leaving these debates largely within conservative media for now.

sources.title: Pauline Hanson: straight to number one
Scrapping jury trials will cost us all
Free online therapy from a headache-inducing government

au-politics.headline: Chinese AI Aids Iran Targeting as US Pilot Rescue Details Emerge
au-politics.body:
ABC reporting reveals that Chinese artificial intelligence is being used to help Iran target US forces in the Middle East with what sources describe as significantly improved precision. The claim points to satellite intelligence sharing between Beijing and Tehran, raising questions about the scope of that strategic partnership and its implications for Australian allies.

Separately, details have emerged of a deception operation that successfully extracted a downed US fighter pilot from Iranian territory. The rescue involved coordinated misdirection of Iranian forces and highlights the active and dangerous nature of current US-Iran tensions.

Neither story has drawn direct comment from Australian government ministers in the past 24 hours. Both carry implications for Australia's alliance commitments and its relationship with China, issues that remain live in the lead-up to the federal election.

sources.title: Chinese AI helping Iran target US forces with 'incredible precision'
Deception campaign key to rescue of downed US fighter pilot from Iran

world.headline: US-Israel War on Iran Enters Week Six With No End in Sight
world.body:
Now on day 38, US and Israeli strikes have hit Iran's top university, killing 34 people, while pharmaceutical factories and civilian infrastructure face sustained bombardment. Doctors warn the destruction of drug producers is cutting off patients from life-saving medicines. Pakistan has proposed a two-phase ceasefire, but Iran says it will not reopen the Strait of Hormuz under a temporary deal - and is now threatening to close the Bab al-Mandeb strait, a chokepoint for global trade.

A US special operations mission rescued an American air officer from inside Iran, a rare ground operation that Trump publicly confirmed. He also claimed the US armed Iranian dissidents through Kurdish intermediaries - a statement Kurdish groups flatly denied. Iran says the admission validates its earlier claims about foreign-backed unrest.

sources.title: In maps and photos: Five Iranian civilian bridges at risk of US strikes
Bombing of Iran's pharma producers threatens patients' lives
How US operation to rescue air officer from Iran unfolded
How rescue of US airman in remote part of Iran unfolded
Has Trump confirmed Iran’s claim that protesters were US-armed?
Iran threatens Bab al-Mandeb closure: How would that affect world trade?
Pakistan offers ‘two-phased’ truce deal to end US-Israel war on Iran
Trump’s ‘Bridge Day’ threat: Can a last-ditch ceasefire plan work?
Trump says US armed Iranian dissidents via Kurds, Kurdish groups deny claim
Iran’s top university bombed as US, Israel intensify attacks; 34 killed
Iran war: What is happening on day 38 of US-Israeli attacks?

world.headline: Israel Escalates in Lebanon, Displacing Over a Million People
world.body:
Israeli strikes have destroyed towns across southern Lebanon and hit areas around Beirut previously designated as safe zones. More than 1.2 million people have been forced to flee, with families taking shelter in mountain villages and makeshift refuges. The scale of displacement is now drawing comparisons to the worst phases of the 2006 war.

Yemen's Houthis have formally entered the conflict on Iran's side, deepening fears among Yemeni civilians about retaliatory strikes on their already devastated country. Meanwhile, Gaza's economy remains paralysed under Israeli control, leaving young Palestinians with no work and no exit.

sources.title: Israel destroys southern Lebanon towns, hits ‘safe’ areas around Beirut
Up to 1.2 million people forced to flee as Israel pummels Lebanon
Families fleeing Israeli attacks take refuge in Lebanon’s mountains
Yemen’s civilians fear fallout after Houthis enter Iran war
Jobless young Palestinians trapped as Israel holds Gaza’s economy hostage

world.headline: Ukraine Strikes Russian Refineries as War Reaches St Petersburg
world.body: Ukrainian drone and missile attacks on Russian oil refineries are now close enough to St Petersburg that residents report smelling smoke and fuel in the air. The strikes mark a psychological shift - war, long a distant headline for Russia's second city, is becoming tangible. The targeting of energy infrastructure is part of a broader Ukrainian strategy to squeeze Russian fuel supplies and military logistics.

sources.title: ‘Smell’ of war comes to St Petersburg as Ukraine hammers Russian refineries

world.headline: Bangladesh Measles Deaths and Nigeria Church Attack Among Global Crises
world.body:
Bangladesh has launched emergency vaccination drives after at least 100 children died from suspected measles within a single month, exposing gaps in routine immunisation coverage. The outbreak has alarmed health officials across South Asia.

In Nigeria, the army rescued 31 people taken hostage following an attack on a church over Easter. The DR Congo, meanwhile, agreed to accept deportees from the United States - a deal that follows similar US pressure on other African governments to take back nationals facing removal.

sources.title: Emergency jabs after 100 children die of suspected measles in a month in Bangladesh
Nigerian army rescues 31 held after Easter church attack
DR Congo agrees to take deportees from the US

ai.headline: ChatGPT Gets Real-World Hooks With DoorDash, Uber, Spotify
ai.body:
OpenAI has rolled out app integrations that let ChatGPT take direct action inside services like DoorDash, Uber, and Spotify - ordering food, booking rides, and controlling music without leaving the chat interface. It is a meaningful shift from ChatGPT as a question-answering tool toward something closer to an autonomous agent layer sitting on top of everyday apps.

The timing is notable. Microsoft's Copilot terms of service quietly describe the product as being "for entertainment purposes only," a legal hedge that undercuts its positioning as a serious productivity tool. OpenAI, by contrast, is moving in the opposite direction - embedding itself deeper into commercial transactions where utility is hard to dispute.

sources.title: How to use the new ChatGPT app integrations, including DoorDash, Spotify, Uber, and others
Copilot is ‘for entertainment purposes only,’ according to Microsoft’s terms of use

ai.headline: Google's Gemma 4 Runs Locally on Phones and Laptops
ai.body:
Google's Gemma 4 model is now running on consumer hardware, with developers reporting successful local deployments on iPhones via the AI Edge Gallery app and on Apple Silicon Macs using LM Studio's new headless CLI. Separate projects are already building on top of it - one demo shows real-time audio and video input with voice output running entirely on an M3 Pro chip.

The pace of on-device AI is accelerating fast. A browser-embedded version of the earlier Gemma model, requiring no API keys or cloud connection, also surfaced this week. Taken together, these projects signal that capable open models are moving off servers and onto personal devices at a speed that is starting to outrun enterprise deployment cycles.

sources.title: Gemma 4 on iPhone
Show HN: Real-time AI (audio/video in, voice out) on an M3 Pro with Gemma E2B
Running Gemma 4 locally with LM Studio's new headless CLI and Claude Code
Show HN: Gemma Gem – AI model embedded in a browser – no API keys, no cloud

ai.headline: Intel's Chip Packaging Bet and Xoople's $130M Earth-Mapping Play
ai.body:
Intel is wagering that advanced chip packaging - stacking and connecting chiplets in novel ways - could become the decisive battleground in the AI hardware race, potentially worth billions if it can execute. It is an unglamorous bet compared to raw silicon, but one that could matter enormously as AI chips push against physical design limits.

Meanwhile, Spanish startup Xoople closed a $130 million Series B to build high-resolution geospatial data infrastructure specifically for AI training and inference. The round reflects growing demand for structured, real-world data that goes beyond text - maps, terrain, and physical-world context that models increasingly need to be useful outside a chat window.

sources.title: The Ridiculously Nerdy Intel Bet That Could Rake in Billions
Spain’s Xoople raises $130 million Series B to map the Earth for AI

ai.headline: AI Reshapes Small Sellers, Suno Faces Copyright Heat
ai.body:
AI tools from platforms like Alibaba's Accio are changing how small online merchants decide what to make and sell, using demand forecasting and trend analysis that was previously available only to large retailers. The shift is compressing the advantage that scale once provided in product planning.

On the creative side, Suno is drawing sharp scrutiny over copyright. The AI music generator can produce covers and derivative works that sit in a legal grey zone, and rights holders are pushing back. The core tension - whether AI-generated music that closely mimics an artist's style constitutes infringement - remains unresolved, and Suno's rapid growth is forcing the question into the open faster than courts or legislators are ready to answer it.

sources.title: AI is changing how small online sellers decide what to make
Suno is a music copyright nightmare

btc.headline: Bitcoin Reclaims $69K as Iran Ceasefire Hopes Squeeze Shorts
btc.body:
Bitcoin surged back above $69,000 Monday after reports of Iran ceasefire talks triggered a wave of short liquidations. Prediction markets had been tracking Iran war bets as a real-time macro signal, and the diplomatic shift flipped sentiment fast. Algorand and other altcoins extended gains alongside BTC.

The rally arrives with a catch. One analyst warns a failure to reclaim $75,000 keeps a drop to $10,000 on the table, while a separate CoinDesk outlook flags inflation data this week as the next major test. The bounce is real, but the structural picture remains fragile.

sources.title: Bitcoin has room to rally, but there's a catch
Bitcoin rallies on report of Iran ceasefire talks, Algorand extends gains
First real bull signal since 2025? Five things to know in Bitcoin this week
Iran war bets turn prediction markets into real-time macro radar: Sygnum
Bitcoin reclaims $69,000 as ceasefire talks surface and crypto shorts get squeezed
Bitcoin meltdown to $10,000 remains likely unless prices reclaim $75,000, analyst says
Trump-Iran deadline chaos sends crypto higher while ceasefire hopes rise

btc.headline: Strategy Buys $330M More Bitcoin, Bitmine Stacks 4.8M ETH
btc.body:
Strategy purchased another 4,871 bitcoin for $330 million, pushing its total holdings close to 767,000 BTC. The buy came after a weeklong pause that had prompted speculation from Michael Saylor himself about the next purchase. Institutional accumulation is accelerating on both sides of the market.

Bitmine is making a parallel bet on Ethereum. The company's ether treasury hit 4.8 million ETH and its stock is moving to the NYSE, signaling that the corporate treasury playbook pioneered with Bitcoin is now being replicated at scale with ETH.

sources.title: Bitmine's ether treasury hits 4.8 million ETH as stock listing moves to NYSE
Strategy added another 4,871 bitcoin for $330 million, with holdings nearing 767,000 BTC
Michael Saylor hints at next Strategy Bitcoin buy after weeklong pause

btc.headline: Circle Bets on Quantum Resistance, Samson Mow Urges Caution
btc.body:
Circle unveiled a quantum-resistant roadmap for its new layer-1 blockchain Arc, building post-quantum cryptography in from the start rather than retrofitting it later. The move positions Arc as a stablecoin-native chain built for a threat most networks are still ignoring.

Not everyone thinks speed is the answer. Samson Mow warned that rushing a quantum fix onto Bitcoin could backfire, arguing that a poorly implemented upgrade carries its own serious risks. The debate cuts to a core tension: act early and risk a bad patch, or wait and risk being caught unprepared.

sources.title: Rushed quantum fix may backfire for Bitcoin, Samson Mow warns
Circle future-proofs Arc blockchain against quantum computing threats
Circle unveils quantum-resistant roadmap for its layer-1 blockchain Arc

btc.headline: North Korea, Deepfake KYC Tools, and China's Bitchat Ban
btc.body:
A researcher revealed that North Korean operatives have been embedding themselves inside DeFi protocols for seven years, dating back to DeFi Summer. Separately, a new AI cybercrime tool is now targeting crypto and bank KYC systems using deepfakes, raising the stakes for identity verification across the industry.

China added its own pressure point, ordering Apple to pull Jack Dorsey's Bitchat from its Chinese App Store. The encrypted messaging app had seen heavy use during Iran protests, making it a clear target for Beijing. The removal underscores how crypto-adjacent communication tools are increasingly caught in geopolitical crossfire.

sources.title: New AI cybercrime tool targets crypto, bank KYC systems via deepfakes
China orders Apple to pull Dorsey's Bitchat, the messaging app used during Iran protests
North Korean workers have been infiltrating DeFi for 7 years: Researcher
Apple pulls Jack Dorsey's Bitchat from China at Beijing’s request

food.name: Miso-Glazed Cabbage Steaks
food.description: Thick-cut cabbage roasted until caramelised and lacquered with a sweet-salty miso and mirin glaze
food.tip: Press the cabbage slices dry with a cloth before roasting — moisture is the enemy of the char you're after

exercise.name: Monday Reset: Loaded Carry Circuit
exercise.equipment: Two kettlebells or heavy shopping bags, a clear corridor or outdoor path
exercise.description: Alternate between farmer carries, suitcase carries, and overhead carries across 20-metre lengths, resting only between variations — builds grip, core stability, and real-world strength in a way machines never replicate

learn.description: A 15th-century illustrated codex written in an entirely unknown script and language that has defeated cryptographers, linguists, and AI analysis for over a century — its 240 vellum pages depict unidentifiable plants, naked figures in green pools, and astronomical diagrams
learn.why: A 2025 statistical analysis suggested the text has genuine linguistic structure rather than being nonsense, meaning someone, somewhere, encoded something real — and on a quiet Monday it's a genuinely humbling reminder that some puzzles still beat us
